Ptilotus cichorioides, commonly known as 'pound cake' or 'devil's pickle', is a species of flowering plant in the family Amaranthaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine, with reported health benefits including the treatment of various gastrointestinal issues and the reduction of inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Ptilotus cichorioides include sesquiterpenoids and triterpenoids, which have been shown to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal medicine, Ptilotus cichorioides is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is sometimes combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
